mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value 109.5645 for 2019-05-01 indicates above-trend output
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not positive in greater than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.5% represents restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and debt-service costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage at 2.39% as of 2019-05-01 sits just below the 2.5% threshold
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in less than 45% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) jointly signal a broad contraction in aggregate demand and labor income that reduces expected corporate revenues, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage 151095 and INDPRO 109.5645 as of 2019-05-01 show mixed but non-contracting levels
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month declines in PAYEMS and INDPRO fail to produce negative 60-day forward returns in greater than 50% of joint-decline months over any 5-year rolling window, or interaction term p-value exceeds 0.05 in a bivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.2 percentage points over two months signals a turning point in labor market tightness that precedes earnings downgrades, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vintage at 3.6% as of 2019-05-01 is near multi-decade lows consistent with late-cycle conditions
failure_condition: During late-cycle periods with UNRATE rising more than 0.2pp in 2 months, 5-day forward returns are negative in less than 40% of triggered instances, or the signal Sharpe ratio over a 2-year rolling window falls below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer spending momentum that translates into higher expected corporate top-line growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value 519020 for 2019-05-01 supports robust nominal spending
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS changes are not followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in greater than 55% of cases over any 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) reflect broad-based demand strength across interest-rate-sensitive and consumer sectors, reinforcing expectations of sustained economic growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage 1235 and RSAFS 519020 as of 2019-04/2019-05 show resilient demand components
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in HOUST and RSAFS fail to produce positive 20-day forward returns in greater than 55% of joint-increase months over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 2 percent over a rolling 3-month window signals acute manufacturing contraction and inventory liquidation that depresses aggregate earnings estimates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage 109.5645 for 2019-05-01 remains above recent troughs but is vulnerable to threshold breach
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines more than 2% over 3 months, 20-day forward returns are negative in less than 45% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In the post-recession recovery phase, quarterly real GDP (GDPC1) growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized confirms escape velocity from the downturn, reducing equity risk premia and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage 18907.517 for 2019-Q1 corresponds to roughly 3.2% annualized growth from prior quarter
failure_condition: During post-recession periods with GDPC1 growth exceeding 2.5% annualized, 60-day forward returns are positive in less than 55% of triggered quarters, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 5-year rolling window.
